headline: What you expect changes what you feel
subtext: The brain predicts pain before the signal even arrives.
caption:
Pain is not a simple readout from the body. The brain is constantly predicting it, and those predictions shape what you actually feel.

Research on expectation shows that the same stimulus hurts more when we expect it to, and less when we expect relief. The brain behaves like a prediction machine, weighing prior experience and belief alongside the incoming signal, in regions that govern attention and emotion.

This is central to neuroplastic symptoms. In chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID, expectations of harm can amplify real symptoms, which means shifting those expectations is a genuine route to relief.

Explore this with a practitioner at https://neurowaze.health/start-my-recovery

#ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: The review: Atlas LY and Wager TD (2012), How expectations shape pain, Neuroscience Letters, 520(2), 140 to 148. Expectations measurably alter both subjective pain and its processing in the brain, consistent with a predictive model of pain. https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0304394012004090
